content: The prefrontal-hippocampal system’s dynamic interaction during retrieval to re-compress and update existing memories can inspire a new adaptive memory architecture for long-horizon AI agents. In this framework, a prefrontal-inspired 'summarization controller' triggers context-dependent hippocampal-inspired memory re-compression only during retrieval of relevant old memories, rather than running compression as a static offline step, to better retain long-term task-relevant knowledge.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
trigger_topic: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
